title: Dubrovnik 10-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Dubrovnik 10-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 10-day trip to Dubrovnik, Croatia: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Dubrovnik City Walls walk and Mount Srđ cable car. Prices from $1816 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 10 days discovering Dubrovnik, Croatia — Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 29-30°C) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 13°C). May-June and September, for warm weather and thinner crowds than the July-August peak. This itinerary blends Dubrovnik City Walls walk, Mount Srđ cable car, Stradun (Placa) with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
9 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Dubrovnik City Walls walk, Mount Srđ cable car, Stradun (Placa)
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Dubrovnik
itinerary.description: Land at Dubrovnik Airport (DBV) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Dubrovnik City Walls walk — Nearly 2km walk atop the medieval fortifications ringing the Old Town. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Dubrovnik: Mount Srđ cable car & Rector's Palace
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Mount Srđ cable car — Cable car to a hilltop viewpoint over the Old Town and Adriatic. In the afternoon, continue to Rector's Palace — Gothic-Renaissance palace museum, former seat of the Ragusan Republic. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Dubrovnik: Lokrum Island ferry and reserve & Game of Thrones filming locations tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Lokrum Island ferry and reserve — Short boat ride to a nature reserve island with peacocks and a botanical garden. In the afternoon, continue to Game of Thrones filming locations tour — Guided walk to Old Town sites used as King's Landing. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Game of Thrones filming locations tour

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Elafiti Islands boat trip
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Elafiti Islands boat trip, a popular side trip from Dubrovnik. Return to Dubrovnik in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Elafiti Islands boat trip

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Lokrum Island's Dead Sea saltwater lake — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Lokrum Island's Dead Sea saltwater lake

itinerary.title: Dubrovnik: Stradun (Placa) & Old Town Harbour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Stradun (Placa) — The Old Town's limestone-paved main promenade. In the afternoon, continue to Old Town Harbour — Small historic harbor beside the city walls. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Kotor, Montenegro
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Kotor, Montenegro, a popular side trip from Dubrovnik. Return to Dubrovnik in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Dubrovnik: Buža Bar cliffside views & Franciscan Monastery cloister
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Buža Bar cliffside views — Free to admire the cliffs even without ordering a drink. In the afternoon, continue to Franciscan Monastery cloister — Quiet Gothic cloister with one of Europe's oldest pharmacies. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Trsteno Arboretum — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Dubrovnik City Walls walk or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to DBV for your departure flight.

travelTips: Walk the City Walls first thing in the morning or in the last two hours before closing to avoid both crowds and midday heat.
Book Old Town accommodation carefully — cruise-ship days (check the port schedule) bring huge crowds by midday.
A Dubrovnik Card can bundle City Walls entry with museums and transit if you're sightseeing heavily.
Wear proper shoes — the polished stone streets get slippery, especially after rain.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Comfortable, grippy shoes for the polished-limestone Old Town streets
Strong sun protection for the exposed City Walls walk
thingsToDo: Game of Thrones filming locations tour
Sea kayaking around the Old Town walls
Hiking Mount Srđ instead of the cable car
thingsToAvoid: Rolling a wheeled suitcase through the Old Town — it's restricted and noisy on the stone streets.
Visiting the City Walls at midday in July-August — heat and crowds both peak.
Assuming beaches are sandy — most of the Dalmatian coast is pebble or rock.
topRestaurants: Panorama Restaurant — Croatian with Mount Srđ views ($$$)
Peskarija — Casual seafood by the harbor ($$)
hiddenGems: Lokrum Island's Dead Sea saltwater lake
Fort Lovrijenac views from outside the walls
adventureActivities: Sea kayaking around the Old Town walls
Hiking Mount Srđ instead of the cable car
luxuryExperiences: Private yacht day trip to the Elafiti Islands
Suite stay overlooking the Old Town harbor
Private sunset City Walls tour after closing hours
honeymoonExperiences: Private sailing trip along the Dalmatian coast
Romantic dinner at Nautika overlooking the sea
budgetExperiences: Free Stradun and Old Town harbor wander
localEtiquette: The Old Town has rules against rolling suitcase wheels and loud noise after certain hours — respect posted signage.
Dress modestly when visiting churches and monasteries.
Tipping around 10% is appreciated, not strictly mandatory.
localTransportGuide: The Old Town is fully pedestrian and walkable, but note the city walls walk involves stairs and no shade — go early or late in summer. Local buses connect the Old Town to Lapad and other beaches; taxis and rideshare cover trips to and from the airport.
bestTimeToVisit: May-June and September, for warm weather and thinner crowds than the July-August peak.
weather: Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 29-30°C) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 13°C).
visaInformation: Schengen area — visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period for US, UK, Canadian, Australian, and EU passport holders. Other nationalities, including India, should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~9.7h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Dubrovnik?
faqs.answer: Schengen area — visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period for US, UK, Canadian, Australian, and EU passport holders. Other nationalities, including India, should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Dubrovnik?
faqs.answer: May-June and September, for warm weather and thinner crowds than the July-August peak. Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 29-30°C) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 13°C).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Dubrovnik safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Dubrovnik is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Dubrovnik?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EUR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Grace Sharma
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Stradun (Placa) and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Wei Wang
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Old Town Harbour in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: David Williams
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Dubrovnik is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Stradun (Placa). The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Olivia Garcia
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Our trip to Dubrovnik was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Old Town Harbour was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
